title: acaena × lividella
intro: Acaena × lividella, also known as the sheep's bit or white's acaena, is a hybrid flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to New Zealand and Australia. The leaves and stems of this plant contain phenolic acids, flavonoids, and triterpenoid saponins, which exhibit various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acaena × lividella include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Acaena × lividella is sometimes used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, but its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
